fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Oregon Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Oregon - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity oregon, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to oregon, respiratory therapist compact oregon, oregon respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Oregon Medical Board — RT
board.mailing_address:
Oregon Medical Board
1500 SW 1st Ave, Suite 620
Portland, OR 97201-5825

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) exam

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Oregon.

steps.title: Register for an Applicant Portal Account
steps.description: Navigate to the Oregon Medical Board's Applicant Portal (omb.license.acendre.com), click 'Register Now,' and follow the prompts to create your account and verify your email address.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your Applicant Portal account, select the Respiratory Therapist application, and meticulously fill out all required sections including personal, education, and professional history.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload all necessary documents such as official transcripts from an approved RT program, NBRC credentials verification (e.g., via NBRC Score Transfer Service), and any other forms or authorizations requested through the Applicant Portal.

steps.title: Pay Application Fees
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable initial application fee ($100) and the background check fee ($52) directly through the Applicant Portal using a credit card.

steps.title: Undergo Background Check
steps.description: Complete and submit to the fingerprint-based criminal background check process as instructed by the Oregon Medical Board after submitting your application.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check your application status within the Applicant Portal and respond promptly to any requests for additional information or clarification from the Oregon Medical Board.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Oregon respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Oregon have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Oregon. To work in Oregon, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Oregon?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Oregon while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Oregon
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Therapist license in Oregon, you must create an account and complete the application through the Oregon Medical Board's Applicant Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Go to the Applicant Portal and click 'Register Now'. You will need to provide your email, personal information (name, address, DOB, last 4 SSN, mother's maiden name), create a username and password, and then confirm your registration via email.
